ticket.summary: As a parent, I want to see my 14-year-old's full medical record including mental health notes so I can monitor their health
ticket.description: Parents should have full access to all medical records for their minor children including clinical notes, mental health records, STI test results, and prescription history. The parent should be able to download a full PDF of the record at any time. Access should not require any additional verification beyond the existing proxy login. The feature should send the parent an email every time a new result or note is added to their child's record with the full details in the email body. There is no age restriction on this access.

issuetype.description: Stories track functionality or features expressed as user goals.
issuetype.name: Story

content.text: Parents should have full access to all medical records for their minor children including clinical notes, mental health records, STI test results, and prescription history. The parent should be able to download a full PDF of the record at any time. Access should not require any additional verification beyond the existing proxy login. The feature should send the parent an email every time a new result or note is added to their child's record with the full details in the email body. There is no age restriction on this access.

rawFields.summary: As a parent, I want to see my 14-year-old's full medical record including mental health notes so I can monitor their health

assumed.claim: Parents should have full access to all medical records for their minor children
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation explicitly states 'Proxy and delegate access must be scoped to minimum necessary data — not full record access.' Full record access for proxy/parent is non-compliant. PM must redefine scope to minimum necessary data only.

assumed.claim: Parents should have access to clinical notes for their 14-year-old
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation explicitly states 'Clinical notes, mental health records, STI results, and substance abuse records are off-limits to proxies for this age group' (minors aged 12-17). PM must remove clinical notes from parent proxy access for this age group.

assumed.claim: Parents should have access to mental health records for their 14-year-old
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation explicitly states 'Clinical notes, mental health records, STI results, and substance abuse records are off-limits to proxies for this age group' (minors aged 12-17). PM must remove mental health records from parent proxy access.

assumed.claim: Parents should have access to STI test results for their 14-year-old
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation explicitly states 'Clinical notes, mental health records, STI results, and substance abuse records are off-limits to proxies for this age group' (minors aged 12-17). PM must remove STI results from parent proxy access.

assumed.claim: Parents should have access to prescription history for their 14-year-old
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation states proxy access must be scoped to minimum necessary data per state law for minors aged 12-17. Prescription history access for parents of minors in this age group is not confirmed as permissible. PM must obtain legal/compliance sign-off before including.

assumed.claim: The parent should be able to download a full PDF of the record at any time
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Full record download contradicts the HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard and the restriction on proxy access to sensitive minor records. PM must remove or significantly restrict this feature to compliant data only.

assumed.claim: Access should not require any additional verification beyond the existing proxy login
assumed.pmAction: Documentation states 'Identity verification: Email invite plus identity verification at enrollment. Shared email blocked.' No confirmation that proxy login alone is sufficient for accessing sensitive records. PM must confirm whether additional verification is required for proxy record access.

assumed.claim: The feature should send the parent an email every time a new result or note is added to their child's record with the full details in the email body
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Sending full clinical details including restricted records (mental health, STI, clinical notes) via email to a parent proxy violates HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard and minor confidentiality protections. PM must redesign notification to exclude restricted data categories and confirm minimum necessary scope.

assumed.claim: There is no age restriction on this access
assumed.reason: Contradicts HIPAA constraints. PM must resolve before sprint.
assumed.pmAction: Documentation explicitly states 'Parent access to minor (12-17) records must be restricted per state law.' The ticket's claim of no age restriction directly contradicts this. PM must apply appropriate age-based restrictions.

Documentation states 'Any ticket that does not address the relevant constraint will be returned to the PM before sprint commitment' and 'Every ticket touching these areas must explicitly address the relevant edge case or it will be considered incomplete.' Acceptance criteria are required. PM must define acceptance criteria addressing HIPAA constraints, minor confidentiality, notification scope, and delay handling before this ticket can be committed to a sprint.

structuralIssues: No out of scope items defined
Ambiguous scope — uses words like 'all', 'any', and 'no age restriction' without defining boundaries
Technical prescription — specifies implementation details such as PDF download mechanism, email delivery method, and proxy login system rather than describing desired outcomes
Missing obvious edge cases — no handling defined for minors' right to confidential care (mental health, STI, reproductive health) which varies by jurisdiction and age
Missing obvious edge cases — no consideration of what happens when the minor turns 18 and access must be revoked
Missing obvious edge cases — no handling for situations where a clinician has flagged records as sensitive or confidential from guardians
Missing obvious edge cases — no handling for separated or divorced parents with differing custody and access rights
Missing obvious edge cases — no consideration of HIPAA minor privacy exceptions which may legally prohibit parental access to certain record types
Missing obvious edge cases — no handling for cases where the minor is an emancipated minor
Ambiguous scope — 'full medical record' and 'full details in the email body' are undefined and could expose highly sensitive clinical information without appropriate safeguards

refinedTicket.summary: As a parent or legal guardian, I want to access my minor child's medical records through my proxy account so I can stay informed about their health
refinedTicket.description: Parents and legal guardians with an active proxy relationship should be able to view and download their minor child's medical records from within their existing proxy account access. This includes clinical notes, test results, and prescription history, subject to applicable restrictions based on the child's age, record sensitivity, custody status, and whether certain record types are legally protected from parental access. Access should reflect the correct scope of records permitted at any given time, and parents should be able to receive notifications when new information is added to their child's record. This ticket does not define the legal rules governing which record types are accessible — those rules must be confirmed before implementation begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a parent has an active proxy relationship with a minor child, when they access the child's record, then they can only view record types that are permitted based on the child's age and applicable access rules defined by the product and clinical team

Given a parent attempts to access a record type that has been flagged as restricted or confidential by a clinician, when they view the child's record, then that restricted content is not visible to the parent
Given a parent is viewing their child's record, when they request a download of the record, then only the permitted and non-restricted records are included in the downloaded output
Given a minor child reaches the age of 18, when their birthday is reached, then the parent's proxy access to their medical records is automatically revoked

Given a parent has opted in to record notifications, when a new result or clinical note is added to the child's record, then the parent receives a notification that contains no sensitive clinical detail in the notification body itself

Given a proxy relationship exists, when the system evaluates access, then it applies access rules based on the specific parent-child relationship on record, not a blanket access grant to all proxy users
Given an emancipated minor's status has been recorded in the system, when a parent attempts to access their record, then access is denied regardless of any existing proxy relationship
outOfScope: Defining the legal or clinical rules that determine which record types parents may access by jurisdiction or age — this must be confirmed by clinical, legal, and compliance stakeholders before implementation
Building the proxy login or account linking system — this ticket assumes that system already exists
Determining how custody or divorce documentation is verified or stored
Defining how emancipated minor status is recorded or verified in the system
Any changes to how clinicians flag records as sensitive or confidential
assumptions: A proxy account relationship mechanism already exists and this feature builds on top of it

The definition of which record types are accessible to parents versus restricted from parental view has not been confirmed and must be defined by clinical, legal, and compliance teams before acceptance criteria can be fully validated
The age at which parental proxy access is revoked has not been confirmed — 18 is used as a placeholder based on standard legal adulthood but must be verified
The mechanism for recording emancipated minor status in the system does not yet exist or has not been confirmed
The rules governing access rights for separated or divorced parents with differing custody arrangements have not been defined and are not addressed in this ticket
Notification delivery method and format have not been confirmed — the acceptance criteria describe the outcome only
No verified facts were available to confirm any compliance, legal, or clinical requirements — all such rules are flagged as assumptions requiring stakeholder confirmation

acceptanceCriteria.revised: Seven acceptance criteria added in Given/When/Then format covering permitted record access, clinician-flagged restrictions, download scope, access revocation at age 18, notification content safety, relationship-specific access grants, and emancipated minor access denial
